checks.a_analysis: The output contains information directly sourced from the PDF pages as evidenced by the OCR. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or section headings were detected. Metadata titles are within acceptable parameters for AI-generated accessibility aids.

All URLs and data points (such as application counts and percentage increases) were verified against the provided OCR text for each page. No instances of fabrication were found. Truncated URLs in the output match the visual representation in the source.

While the provided JSON contains placeholders like '_omitted' for several slides (Pages 5-7, 9, 14-15), the reading_order indices indicate that these sections are accounted for in the conversion pipeline. These omissions appear to be for token budget management in the prompt display rather than failures of the conversion process itself.

All tables present in the output, including the attendance table (page 2), vote tables (page 4), city analytics (page 12), and PSAP impact results (page 16), contain the correct number of rows as per the source PDF.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: In the 'ROI – Effectiveness of the Pilot Program' section (Page 17), the output omits the detailed sub-bullets under item 3 ('Direct – Interested party...', 'Organic Search – PSA pushed...'). This omission constitutes a loss of more than 25% of the textual content for that specific list.
checks.b_evidence_source:
3. Website Analytics – Reporting high traffic with the source coming from direct and organic search.
• Direct – Interested party directly went to the site by typing the URL.
• Organic Search – PSA pushed interested party to website to find out more information.
checks.b_evidence_output: Website Analytics – Reporting high traffic with the source coming from direct and organic search.
checks.b_evidence_location: ROI – Effectiveness of the Pilot Program, Page 17, element index 125

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: In the Networks list on Page 8, the channel 'NICK' is incorrectly transcribed as 'NATG', resulting in a duplicate entry for 'NATG' in that section.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains an agenda, meeting minutes, and a set of presentation slides. While headers like 'North Carolina 911 Board' repeat on multiple pages in the source, this is intentional. The output JSON accurately captures the distinct sections without unnecessary duplication of the body text or table data.

The heading hierarchy is well-maintained throughout the conversion. Major titles for the agenda, minutes, and presentation slides are assigned H1, with sub-sections properly nested as H2 or H3. For example, the 'Durham (RLDU) Zone' slide title is H1, followed by 'Demographics' as a table header and 'Networks' as H2. No heading levels are skipped.

The 'Meeting Attendees' table on Page 2 is structurally corrupted in the conversion. The table is defined with 6 header columns ('Committee Members', 'Phone', 'Not in Attendance', 'Guests', 'Phone', 'In Person'). However, starting with the row for 'Staff' (index 11 in the rows array), the number of cells in each row drops to 3 without any colspan attributes to maintain the 6-column structure defined by the header. This results in an invalid table grid where rows have different lengths.
checks.b_evidence_source: The table on page 2 contains rows for 'Committee Members' (left side) and 'Guests' (right side) side-by-side, followed by 'Staff' rows on the left side.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table at reading_order 13 contains a header row with 6 cells, but rows starting from index 11 (Staff sub-header) only contain 3 cells (e.g., 'Staff', 'Phone', 'Not in Attendance').

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the JSON output follows the logical progression of the source PDF, moving from the meeting agenda to the minutes and finally through the presentation slides. The sequence of information within each page is preserved.

Several numbered lists in the source document have been incorrectly converted to unordered lists, and the leading numbers have been stripped from the text. This occurs in the minutes (items 1-9 across pages 2-4) and the ROI slide (items 1-4 on page 17). This loss of numbering structure degrades the semantic meaning of the lists.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2: '1. Chair Opening Remarks – ...'; Page 3: '4. Approval of July Minutes – ...'; Page 17: '1. Increase in Number of Applications – ...'
checks.b_evidence_output: reading_order 15: '**Chair Opening Remarks** – ...'; reading_order 18: 'Approval of July Minutes – ...'; reading_order 125: 'Increase in Number of Applications – ...' (all inside unordered list items)

checks.a_analysis: The URLs listed on Page 13 ('Top 10 Clicks') are rendered as plain text in the JSON table. While they are not active hyperlinks in the source PDF image, they are clearly visible as URLs. The pipeline has not injected any false links or broken any existing annotations.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document follows the logical sequence of the source PDF. The multi-page agenda, minutes, and presentation slides are converted into sequential web sections. While the layout is single-column (responsive), the visual organization of information remains consistent with the source's intent.

All tables from the source PDF, including the attendee lists in the minutes, the demographics table on page 8, and the complex PSAP Impacts table on page 16, are rendered as functional HTML tables with clear headers and aligned columns. They are fully readable and maintain the data relationships of the original.

Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Specifically, the map of the Durham (RLDU) Zone on page 8, the collage of TV network logos on page 9, and multiple graphical charts (line graph, pie charts, bar chart) in the analytics section (pages 10-12) are absent from the rendered HTML. While some chart data was converted to tables, the visual charts themselves are missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 8: Map of Durham (RLDU) Zone; Page 9: Collage of network logos; Page 10: 'How are site sessions trending?' line chart and 'Desktop vs Mobile' pie chart; Page 12: 'New Visitor vs Returning' pie chart.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML sections for 'Durham (RLDU) Zone - 0664', 'Campaign Airings', 'Webpage Analytics: Site Summary', and 'Website Analytics: Top 20 Cities' are missing their corresponding graphical images/charts.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections corresponding to PDF pages 8, 9, 10, and 12

checks.a_analysis: No body content (paragraphs, table rows, or presentation bullets) is visually duplicated in the HTML output. Section titles appearing in the Table of Contents and again as headings in the body is standard accessible design and does not constitute a defect.
